query: I fit a multiple linear OLS regression of house price on 6 features (size, bedrooms, bathrooms, age, lot_size, neighborhood-fixed-effects) on n = 240 houses. R² = 0.71. The residuals-vs-fitted plot shows a clear fan pattern (spread of residuals grows with fitted price). Run my regression diagnostics and tell me what to do.
expected_behavior: Identifies heteroscedasticity as the primary diagnostic failure (fan pattern in residuals vs fitted, Breusch-Pagan or equivalent named as the confirmatory test)
Recommends heteroscedasticity-consistent (HC1 / HC3 / sandwich / robust / White) standard errors as the remediation, NOT just refitting OLS or dropping observations
Produces or specifies a per-diagnostic verdict table covering at least linearity, Normality of residuals, homoscedasticity, and influence (Cook's D or leverage) — not a single bare verdict
